articles.title: Rain alert in Odisha amid heatwave: IMD Issues warning for multiple districts - Odisha TV
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued yellow weather warnings for several districts of Odisha, indicating continued rainfall and thunderstorm activity across the state over the next few days.
Despite the weakening of the system, active weather conditions are expected to persist. Light to moderate rainfall along with thunderstorms was seen across multiple coastal and interior districts, signalling the onset of widespread rain activity.
Warnings Across Districts
Warnings have been issued for Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar, Bhadrak and Balasore, where thunderstorms accompanied by lightning, hailstorms and gusty winds reaching 30-40 kmph are likely at isolated places. Yellow warnings have also been sounded for parts of coastal Odisha, with wind speeds expected between 30–50 kmph.

Yellow warnings remain in place for lightning and gusty winds up to 40–50 kmph in both northern and southern parts of the state.
Gradual Reduction, Advisory Issued
From April 3 to April 5, the intensity of rainfall is likely to reduce gradually, becoming more localised. Southern districts including Koraput, Malkangiri, Nuapada and Nabarangpur are expected to witness continued thunderstorm activity with winds reaching 30–40 kmph under yellow warning.
The IMD has cautioned about possible damage to crops, banana plantations and kutcha houses, especially in districts under orange alert. Residents have been advised to stay indoors during thunderstorms, avoid sheltering under trees, and take necessary precautions, while farmers have been urged to regulate harvesting and adopt protective measures.
